MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jul-16-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

STONE & ASSOCIATES, ATTY. SEENA R. TAALOMI'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL - CIVIL.

Counsel Seena R Taalomi and Stone & Associates, APC's unopposed Motion to be Relieved as Counsel for Defendant Waddle In, Inc. is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

In the papers counsel states trial in this case that has been pending for 32 months (and pending against counsel's client for 24 months) is set for August 11, 2025-four weeks from now. The court generally will not authorize counsel's withdraw so late in the proceedings and so close to trial. Counsel's motion includes no information that would except this case from the general rule. If counsel or a party obtains a continuance of the trial date, counsel may renew their motion assuming it is noticed for hearing sufficiently in advance of the new trial date.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-21-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
ALEX B. LEMBERG, ESQ., 415) 921-9300 alex@alexlemberg.com for Plaintiff Wyatt Payne,
Mark R. Wilson, Esq., MANNING & KASS ELLROD, RAMIREZ, TRESTER LLP, (415) 217-6990 mark.wilson@manningkass.com for Defendant, VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as followss:

DEFENDANT VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC.'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT

Defendant Vanguard Properties, Inc's demurrer is overruled.

The demurrer to the first cause of action for breach of contract is overruled. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts that defendant entered into and breached an agreement with plaintiff. (First Amended Complaint ("FAC"), pars. 10-17, 24; Ex. A [authorizing plaintiff to use his credit card for purchases "on behalf of Vanguard Property Management"].) Defendant raises a factual issue regarding the scope of the agreement and the court defers to plaintiff's interpretation of the agreement on demurrer. (Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239 ["[W]e must accept as correct plaintiff's allegations as to the meaning of the agreement."]; CCP 452 [liberally construe pleadings].)

The demurrer to the third cause of action (common count-goods services received) is overruled. Liberally reading the FAC, it alleges that the work benefitted defendant as well as Iveli. Defendant again raises a factual issue.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Courts ruling in copliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-21-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
ALEX B. LEMBERG, ESQ., 415) 921-9300 alex@alexlemberg.com for Plaintiff Wyatt Payne,
Mark R. Wilson, Esq., MANNING & KASS ELLROD, RAMIREZ, TRESTER LLP, (415) 217-6990 mark.wilson@manningkass.com for Defendant, VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT

Defendant Vanguard Properties, Inc's motion to strike is granted without leave to amend. The references to Civil Code sec. 8800 and prayers for fees and 2% interest pursuant to that provision are irrelevant.

Paragraph 10 alleges that plaintiff would "perform a wide variety of handyman services" for the distinct properties and those services were "determined on a case-by-case basis." This allegation contradicts the conclusory allegation at paragraph 11 that the work "compromised a single project." Indeed, the allegation at paragraph 11 begs the question when and how the "project" would be complete.

In any event, plaintiff does not plead any facts showing that the 103 invoices constituted "progress payment[s]" within the meaning of Civil Code sec. 8800(a). "[A] progress payment is a payment other than a down payment that is to be made before the project is completed." (Murray's Iron Works, Inc. v. Boyce (2008) 158 Cal.App.4th 1279, 1299.) While Murray's interpreted a predecessor statute, it explained the meaning of the term "progress payment" in the construction industry. (Id.) That definition comports with United Riggers & Erectors, Inc. v. Coast Iron & Steel Co. (2018) 4 Cal.5th 1082, 1087 [Defining "progress payment" under CC 8800 as follows: "Construction contracts often call for payment in installments - known commonly as progress payments - due when a project reaches various stages of completion."].) Plaintiff does not adduce facts that can cure this defect.

The court does not address the credit card interest issue because defendant's notice of motion does not seek to strike the Prayer for Relief, par. 3.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
